name: Sandhills Community Action Program, INC.
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counselor and community action agency serving four NC counties with affordable housing, self-sufficiency programs, and financial guidance for low-income families.
description_long:
Sandhills Community Action Program, Inc. (SCAP) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that has operated for more than 50 years across Anson, Montgomery, Moore, and Richmond Counties in North Carolina. As part of a network of 31 NC community action agencies and over 1,000 nationwide, SCAP is embedded in regional advocacy and direct service delivery to reduce poverty. The organization is led by Executive Director Nina Walker and maintains four county offices to serve residents across the Sandhills region.

SCAP offers a comprehensive suite of services centered on housing stability and economic self-sufficiency. Core offerings include HUD-approved housing counseling (covering homebuying, renting, budgeting, and credit management), administration of the federal Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program for very low-income families and seniors, the Family Self-Sufficiency Program for participants seeking to increase earned income and reduce assistance dependence within five years, the S.T.A.R.S. self-sufficiency program providing education and employment support, senior housing (1- and 2-bedroom apartments for those 62+), home repair assistance for elderly homeowners and veterans through NC Housing Finance Agency funding, and community engagement initiatives.

SCAP distinguishes itself through its deep community integration and governance structure. The organization explicitly requires that one-third of its Board of Directors consist of low-income individuals or their representatives, ensuring that those served have direct voice in organizational direction. This model reflects a philosophy of equity, inclusion, and dignity. Additionally, SCAP's HUD-approved housing counseling status provides credibility for homebuying guidance and credit/budget management services, while its Section 8 administration connects it directly to federal affordable housing infrastructure.

SCAP is appropriate for low-income residents in its four-county service area seeking free housing counseling, rental assistance information, or self-sufficiency programming. However, prospective users should note that services are geographically limited, and the organization's strength is in housing and employment pathways rather than credit repair or emergency cash solutions. Those needing immediate debt relief or credit dispute services would be better served by specialized agencies.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency providing credible guidance on homebuying, renting, budgeting, and credit management
Administers federal Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program, connecting very low-income families and seniors to rental subsidies
Free S.T.A.R.S. self-sufficiency program offering education, certifications, and employment support
Board structure requires one-third low-income representation, ensuring client voice in organizational governance
Multi-county presence with four regional offices and 50+ years of operational history in service area
Family Self-Sufficiency Program supports Section 8 participants in achieving earned income goals and reducing assistance dependence
Home repair assistance for elderly, veterans, and disabled homeowners through state funding partnerships
cons: Services limited to four North Carolina counties (Anson, Montgomery, Moore, Richmond) — no broader geographic reach
Primary focus on housing and self-sufficiency; does not offer credit repair, debt settlement, or emergency cash products
Website does not clearly specify eligibility income thresholds or application processes for individual programs
Senior housing limited to Carthage location with unspecified unit availability
No indication of virtual/remote counseling options, requiring in-person visits to county offices
best_for: Low-income renters in the four-county service area seeking Section 8 housing assistance or rental counseling
First-time homebuyers needing HUD-approved housing counseling and credit/budget guidance
Section 8 participants with income-growth goals seeking five-year self-sufficiency planning
Elderly homeowners (62+) and veterans needing free home repair assistance
diagnosis: SCAP is best for low-income residents in its four-county North Carolina service area seeking free, non-profit housing counseling, rental assistance information, homeownership guidance, or self-sufficiency programming. The main caveat is geographic limitation and lack of credit repair, debt relief, or emergency lending services—prospective clients outside the service area or needing those products should seek elsewhere.
services: Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program administration
Family Self-Sufficiency Program for rental assistance participants
S.T.A.R.S. self-sufficiency program with education, certifications, and employment support
Affordable senior housing (1- and 2-bedroom apartments for age 62+)
Home repair assistance (ESFR) for elderly, veterans, and disabled homeowners
Urgent minor repair program (URP) in Montgomery and Stanly Counties
Board of Directors recruitment for low-income representatives
Community Needs Assessment and program planning
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 340 Commerce Ave Ste 20, Southern Pines, NC 28387
meta_description: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor serving four NC counties with affordable housing, self-sufficiency programs, and financial guidance.
